title: Phaca sesquiflora
intro: Phaca sesquiflora, commonly known as the Wild Bean or Lupin, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Phaca sesquiflora include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Phaca sesquiflora is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Phaca sesquiflora is classified under the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae. Within the genus Phaca, it is a species of flowering plants native to the Iberian Peninsula. The species name 'sesquiflora' refers to the number of flowers on the stem. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Phaca sesquiflora is a legume with characteristic bean pods.
